mission: Finalize the Phase 0 audience-surface runtime bindings and publication parity so every current audience surface and route family has one explicit live, recovery, blocked, partial, or drifted truth row.

assumptions.statement: seq_127 is the authoritative current join across shells, manifests, design bundles, runtime publication, and local release parity; seq_130 finalizes its row-level live/recovery/writable truth rather than re-inventing that join.
The local release parity row is exact, but current browser truth remains bounded by design lint, accessibility, and browser-posture ceilings; exact parity alone does not reopen calm live posture.

publicationCeilingReasons: One or more frontend manifests remain constrained or recovery-only instead of publishable_live.
Accessibility coverage is still degraded for at least one audience surface.
Design contract lint verdicts are not fully passed across the bundle.

bindingState.publishable_live: Exact runtime, parity, design, accessibility, and browser posture all align for calm live truth.
bindingState.recovery_only: The surface remains visible only for bounded same-shell recovery or guarded operational continuation.
bindingState.partial: The tuple exists and parity is exact, but read-only, guarded, or constrained posture still blocks calm live truth.
bindingState.blocked: The route or surface is explicitly unpublished, frozen, or missing required authority members.
bindingState.drifted: The tuple exists but current runtime/publication truth has drifted and must not present as live.

calmTruthState.allowed: Quiet, trustworthy, calmly live posture is legal for this row.
calmTruthState.suppressed: The surface may render summary or fallback posture only; calm truth is withheld.
calmTruthState.diagnostic_only: The row remains diagnostic and inspectable, but not calmly live or reassuring.

writableTruthState.allowed: Current tuple permits live writable action posture.
writableTruthState.blocked: Writable action posture is suppressed by current parity, design, accessibility, or freeze law.
writableTruthState.recovery_only: Only bounded recovery mutations are legal; ordinary writable posture stays closed.

verdictRules.summary: A publishable-live or writable row must have exact parity, published runtime truth, passing design posture, complete accessibility, and publishable browser posture.
Recovery-only, partial, blocked, and drifted rows must name a governing recovery or freeze disposition instead of collapsing to a generic error page.
Every current shell-claimed route family and every current inventoried audience surface must appear in the final binding catalog, including blocked rows.
Surface calm and writable truth is subordinate to the active ReleasePublicationParityRecord and may not outrun it.

reasonCatalog.description: The published design bundle still carries a pending lint verdict, so calm presentation cannot be claimed.
Accessibility coverage is degraded, so the tuple remains bounded and guarded instead of exact.
Accessibility coverage is blocked, so the surface cannot claim a live fused authority tuple.
The release parity row only permits read-only posture.
The release parity row only permits recovery-only posture.
The inventoried surface is channel-constrained and not browser-visible, so it remains bounded even when the shell family is browser-addressable.
No frontend contract manifest currently publishes this route family.
No audience surface route contract is published for this tuple.
No audience surface runtime binding is published for this tuple.
No design contract publication bundle is currently linked to this tuple.
No projection contract version set is currently linked to this tuple.
No persistent shell contract is published for the shell family required by this tuple.
The tuple cannot name a governing bounded context.
The tuple cannot name a canonical object descriptor for the route family.
Frontend, design, runtime, or parity digests no longer align for the fused tuple.
The derived standalone assistive shell route family exists in the inventory but is not published through a shell, manifest, or runtime binding of its own.

declaredDispositionCatalog.title: Standalone assistive publication blocked
declaredDispositionCatalog.summary: Until a standalone assistive shell, manifest, and runtime binding exist, the route family stays frozen out of direct browser publication.

declaredDispositionCatalog.title: Return to the owning shell
declaredDispositionCatalog.summary: Operators must return to the owning staff shell or governed sidecar because no standalone assistive runtime truth is published yet.

routeFamilies.label: Assistive evaluation, replay, monitoring, and release-control surfaces (derived)

stateReferenceRows.title: Publishable live
stateReferenceRows.summary: Exact parity, calm truth, and writable action are all legal.

stateReferenceRows.title: Recovery only
stateReferenceRows.summary: Only bounded same-shell recovery or fallback action remains legal.

stateReferenceRows.title: Partial
stateReferenceRows.summary: The tuple exists but current live truth is still guarded or read-only.

stateReferenceRows.title: Blocked
stateReferenceRows.summary: A hard publication or authority gap prevents live presentation.

stateReferenceRows.title: Drifted
stateReferenceRows.summary: The tuple exists but must not present as live until drift is cleared.

surfaceAuthorityRows.routeFamilyLabel: Assistive evaluation, replay, monitoring, and release-control surfaces (derived)
surfaceAuthorityRows.notes: Standalone assistive control work is explicitly blocked and must route back to the owning shell until a dedicated shell, manifest, and runtime binding are published. The derived standalone assistive shell has route inventory truth but no seeded shell, manifest, or runtime binding of its own.
